questions.question: Which explorer is credited with the first circumnavigation of the globe?
questions.explanation: Ferdinand Magellan led the expedition that first circumnavigated the Earth (1519–1522), though he died in the Philippines before completing it. Juan Sebastián Elcano completed the voyage. Magellan gets the credit as the expedition's leader.
questions.memory_hook: Magellan mapped the globe — he just didn't finish the trip.

questions.question: What ancient civilization built Machu Picchu?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the mid-15th century, likely as a royal estate for Emperor Pachacuti. It sits 7,970 feet above sea level in the Andes Mountains of Peru and was largely unknown to the outside world until 1911.
questions.memory_hook: Inca in the Andes — Machu Picchu is their mountain crown.

questions.question: How many players are on a standard volleyball team on the court?
questions.explanation: A standard volleyball team has six players on the court at a time: three in the front row and three in the back row. Teams rotate positions after winning the serve.
questions.memory_hook: Six-pack — volleyball has six on the court like a six-pack standing up.

questions.question: What is the maximum score in a single game of ten-pin bowling?
questions.explanation: A perfect game in ten-pin bowling is 300, achieved by bowling 12 consecutive strikes. The 10th frame allows up to three balls if the first two are strikes, enabling 12 total strikes in a game.
questions.memory_hook: 300 — like the movie, it's a perfect battle.

questions.question: Who sang 'Respect', which became an anthem of the civil rights movement?
questions.explanation: Aretha Franklin recorded 'Respect' in 1967, originally written and recorded by Otis Redding. Franklin's version transformed it into an anthem for both the civil rights and feminist movements. She became known as the 'Queen of Soul.'
questions.memory_hook: Aretha R-E-S-P-E-C-T — she spelled it out for us.

questions.question: Which film franchise features a character named 'Jack Sparrow'?
questions.explanation: Captain Jack Sparrow, played by Johnny Depp, is the main character of the Pirates of the Caribbean franchise, which began with 'The Curse of the Black Pearl' in 2003. The character was inspired in part by Rolling Stones guitarist Keith Richards.
questions.memory_hook: Jack Sparrow — sparrow flies free like a pirate.

questions.question: What decade did rock and roll emerge as a mainstream genre?
questions.explanation: Rock and roll emerged in the early 1950s in the United States, blending African American musical styles including blues, boogie woogie, jazz, and gospel. Artists like Chuck Berry, Little Richard, and Elvis Presley brought it to mainstream audiences.
questions.memory_hook: Rock 'n' roll was born in the '50s — when poodle skirts met electric guitars.

questions.question: Which U.S. President signed the Emancipation Proclamation?
questions.explanation: Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation on January 1, 1863, declaring that all enslaved people in Confederate states 'shall be then, thenceforward, and forever free.' It was a key turning point in the Civil War.
questions.memory_hook: Lincoln freed the nation's conscience as much as its people.

questions.question: Mount Everest sits on the border of which two countries?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest straddles the border between Nepal to the south and China (specifically the Tibet Autonomous Region) to the north. At 8,849 meters (29,032 feet), it is the highest point on Earth above sea level. The summit itself is on the border — you can stand in both countries at the top.
questions.memory_hook: Everest = Nepal and China. Two countries share the top of the world. The summit straddles both — literally standing in two places at once.

questions.question: Which gas makes up approximately 78% of Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere, with oxygen at roughly 21% and argon at about 0.93%. Despite breathing air constantly, we use only the oxygen fraction. Nitrogen is mostly inert at normal conditions, which is why it makes up the bulk without reacting with everything around us.
questions.memory_hook: We breathe mostly nitrogen, not oxygen. Nitrogen 78%, oxygen 21%. The atmosphere is mostly a gas we can't actually use.

questions.question: Sri Lanka, the island nation at the southern tip of India, was known as what name under British colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Sri Lanka was called Ceylon under British colonial rule from 1815 until independence in 1948. The country changed its name to Sri Lanka ('Resplendent Island') in 1972 when it became a republic. Sri Lanka is the world's largest producer of cinnamon and one of the top tea producers globally — Ceylon tea remains a famous brand. The island separated from mainland India by the shallow Palk Strait, only about 31 km wide at its narrowest.
questions.memory_hook: Sri Lanka = formerly Ceylon. British colony until 1948. Renamed Sri Lanka in 1972. World's largest cinnamon producer. 'Ceylon tea' was named after it. Separated from India by Palk Strait — only 31 km at the narrowest.

questions.question: Saturn's rings are among the solar system's most spectacular features — but they are also surprisingly young and disappearing. What do scientists now understand about them?
options: Saturn's rings formed with the planet 4.6 billion years ago and are gradually expanding outward — they will eventually escape Saturn's gravity and become a separate moon in about 500 million years
Saturn's rings are relatively young (100–400 million years old) and are actively raining material onto Saturn — losing mass at a rate that could cause them to disappear within 100 million years

Saturn's rings are the densest structure in the solar system — their total mass equals that of Saturn's moon Mimas, and they are composed primarily of iron and nickel particles from an ancient asteroid impact
Saturn's rings are a permanent feature — their material is continuously replenished by volcanic activity on the inner moons Enceladus and Tethys, keeping total ring mass approximately constant
questions.answer: Saturn's rings are relatively young (100–400 million years old) and are actively raining material onto Saturn — losing mass at a rate that could cause them to disappear within 100 million years
questions.explanation: Saturn formed 4.6 billion years ago, but its rings are only 100–400 million years old — meaning they appeared when dinosaurs were already walking on Earth. They likely formed when a moon or large comet was torn apart by Saturn's gravity. The rings are 90–95% water ice and about 20 meters thick on average, yet span 275,000 km in diameter. The Cassini spacecraft's final measurements found 'ring rain' — ring material falling into Saturn's atmosphere at up to 10,000 kg per second. At this rate, the rings could be gone within 100 million years — we may be seeing them at a cosmically rare moment.
questions.memory_hook: Saturn's rings are only 100–400 million years old — they appeared when dinosaurs roamed Earth. Cassini measured 'ring rain' losing 10,000 kg of ice per second into Saturn's atmosphere; in 100 million years, they could be completely gone. We may be seeing them at a cosmically unique moment.
